g.data-narration: Machine Learning sits at the intersection of multiple disciplines, combining artificial intelligence, statistics, computer science, and engineering to create intelligent data-driven systems.
g.text: Machine Learning in Relation to Other Fields

g.data-narration: Artificial Intelligence serves as the parent discipline, encompassing the broader goal of creating machines capable of performing tasks that typically require human intelligence like reasoning.
g.text: The parent field: broad science of creating intelligent machines
Goal: reasoning, problem-solving, perception, language understanding
Covers expert systems, robotics, planning, computer vision

g.data-narration: Machine Learning represents the core methodology within AI, enabling systems to automatically discover patterns in data and improve performance through experience without explicit programming rules.
g.text: The engine: learning from data instead of explicit programming

g.data-narration: Statistics provides the mathematical foundation for ML, offering techniques like regression analysis, hypothesis testing, probability distributions, and statistical inference to validate predictions accurately.
g.text: Mathematical backbone: regression, hypothesis testing, variance, probability
Provides rigor in inference, uncertainty quantification, and model validation

g.data-narration: Data Science is an interdisciplinary field that applies ML techniques within a broader pipeline of data collection, cleaning, visualization, and extracting actionable business insights.
g.text: Uses ML for prediction within data pipeline: collection, cleaning, visualization
Combines statistics, domain expertise, and communication skills for insights

g.data-narration: Computer Science provides the essential infrastructure for ML, including efficient algorithms, data structures, databases, distributed computing frameworks, and software engineering practices to deploy at scale.
g.text: Algorithms, data structures, databases, cloud computing, distributed systems
Software engineering for building scalable ML applications and APIs

g.data-narration: Successful ML practitioners blend mathematical rigor from statistics, computational efficiency from computer science, and practical engineering problem-solving skills to build real-world intelligent systems.
g.text: ML = AI + Statistics + Data Science + Computer Science + Engineering
